mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of months when PAYEMS is increasing, or rank correlation between PAYEMS change and returns is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds restrictive thresholds, elevated real borrowing costs tighten financial conditions and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in unemployment and declines in industrial production jointly signal a contracting business cycle, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint UNRATE-INDPRO signal and 20-day returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, deceleration in retail sales growth signals exhausted consumption momentum and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as corporate guidance weakens.
failure_condition: RSAFS deceleration coincides with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of late-cycle quarters, or the conditional return spread between late-cycle and early-recovery regimes is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production momentum leads near-term corporate earnings surprises and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon as manufacturing strength translates into higher immediate profitability.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of weeks when INDPRO is increasing, or rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and 5-day returns is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation above trend triggers inflation-scare dynamics that raise required equity risk premiums and discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered inflation-scare months, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts lead the broader economy by several quarters; a sustained decline in residential construction activity signals collapsing investment and negative wealth effects that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST declines greater than 10% from a 6-month peak are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the hit rate for negative returns is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between restrictive monetary policy and rising unemployment creates a non-linear drag on corporate cash flows and discount rates, amplifying negative S&P 500 returns over the next month beyond the sum of individual effects.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE change produces rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of joint restrictive-policy rising-unemployment observations.
